decisions.title: Prioritize Canvas GA over Explore V2 family features
decisions.context: Canvas disposition history (ENG-9828) and pass/fail buttons (ENG-9437) are nearing completion while Explore V2 family plotting (ENG-6408-6412) spans multiple panels. Canvas directly supports Signal>Evidence>Decision workflow and captures decisions explicitly (North Star metric).

arguments.for: Canvas features directly increment 'Decisions Captured Per Customer' metric. Pass/fail buttons and disposition history are core decision-capture primitives. Canvas is Embed Deeper pillar - integrating into customer workflows.
arguments.against: Explore V2 family features (ENG-6408-6412, ENG-7789, DES-61) represent significant customer asks. Delaying could impact Nail the Core pillar. Multiple alignment points (ENG-7789) blocks advanced family analysis workflows.

decisions.title: Sunset PTSID service before Sift Edge GA
decisions.context: ENG-9651 removes PTSID service/cache - architectural simplification. Sift Edge (ENG-8857 adds enum/bitfield support) is Build the Moat pillar. Technical debt cleanup should precede new edge capabilities to avoid compounding complexity.
decisions.blockedBy: Migration plan for existing PTSID-dependent integrations unclear. Need customer impact analysis.

arguments.for: Reduces infrastructure complexity before launching edge product. Cleaner foundation for Sift Edge = faster iteration. Aligns with 'Nail the Core' by simplifying architecture.
arguments.against: Adds migration risk pre-GA. Could delay Sift Edge launch if PTSID removal uncovers dependencies. Edge customers may not care about backend simplification.

decisions.title: Address E2 data selector regression (ENG-7289) before broader E2 rollout
decisions.context: Data source swap from E2 selector is broken. ENG-9720 shows E2/E1 campaign jumping issues. E2 represents Explore V2 - core product evolution. Bugs undermine 'Nail the Core' pillar and customer trust during transition period.

arguments.for: E2 adoption blocked by data selector issues. Customers experiencing friction switching between E1/E2 reduces confidence in platform stability. Quick fix preserves momentum on Explore V2 adoption.
arguments.against: E2 is pre-GA so some bugs expected. Resources could focus on new E2 features instead of regression fixes. Workarounds may exist for affected customers.

decisions.title: Standardize alignment UX (ENG-9832, ENG-7666) before multi-alignment feature (ENG-7789)
decisions.context: Auto-naming alignments (ENG-9832) and confusing removal behavior (ENG-7666) indicate baseline alignment UX needs polish. Multi-alignment feature (ENG-7789, DES-61) adds complexity. Foundation must be solid first.

arguments.for: Poor baseline UX amplified by advanced features. Auto-naming and deletion clarity are table-stakes. Fixing now prevents compounding confusion when users manage multiple alignments per family member.
arguments.against: Multi-alignment is higher customer priority (DES-61 explicitly scoped). Delaying advanced feature for polish work risks appearing slow to deliver. Customers may tolerate UX quirks for functionality.

timeAudit.weekSummary: High meeting load (32h) limiting execution bandwidth. Canvas and family features both need focused build time.
alerts: Only 2 focus blocks today - insufficient for deep product strategy work on Canvas vs Explore V2 tradeoffs
22 meetings this week suggests over-collaboration - consider async decision-making for alignment UX priorities
3 one-on-ones today - opportunity to align leads on PTSID migration blockers and E2 regression urgency
